parsed_review.summary: CP00-158 received a valid read-only Claude review with one P2 and four P3 findings, all fixed before closeout.

boundary_assessment.notes: CP00-158 remains descriptor-only and no-write after finding fixes.

findings.title: Customer-safe no-leak guarantee is asserted only on blocked_output, but the tail descriptor embeds the full upstream workflow object
findings.recommended_action: Add an assertion on the full descriptor, e.g. assert.equal(JSON.stringify(denied).includes("must not leak"), false). If it passes, it confirms CP157 workflow redaction and locks the guarantee in. If it fails, sanitize the embedded `workflow` (or drop raw payload fields from it) before returning. Alternatively, if the full tail descriptor is intended as internal-only evidence and only `blocked_output` may cross the customer boundary, document that boundary explicitly in the README/contract so the distinction is not lost in later runtime packs.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: createMasterDataServiceTailDescriptor now sanitizes the embedded workflow recursively and model tests assert the full descriptor does not leak hidden source values.

findings.title: routeFor maps review/approval claim arrays without the null-guard used for blocked_claims
findings.recommended_action: Apply the same `?? []` guard to review_required_claims and approval_required_claims for consistency with the blocked path, or document the invariant that these arrays are always present when the outcome matches.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: review_required_claims and approval_required_claims now use the same null-guard pattern as blocked claims.

findings.title: validateMasterDataCp158TailBoundary spot-checks only 7 of the 10 validation_error_mapping keys
findings.recommended_action: Iterate Object.keys(MASTER_DATA_SERVICE_TAIL_BOUNDARY.validation_error_mapping) instead of a hardcoded subset so the validator stays complete as the mapping grows.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: validateMasterDataCp158TailBoundary now checks all expected mapping claims and validates every configured safe code entry.

findings.title: Customer-facing blocked_output includes raw internal claim names (blocked_claims) alongside safe_error_codes
findings.recommended_action: Decide intentionally whether raw blocked_claims belong in the customer-safe output. If yes (to aid client-side categorization), note it as intended; if the customer surface should be codes-only, move blocked_claims and the raw `claim` keys to an internal-only section of the descriptor.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: customer-facing blocked_output now exposes safe_error_codes only; raw blocked claim refs moved to internal_blocked_claim_refs and README documents the boundary.

findings.title: Overlapping persistence attestation keys: executes_persistence_boundary (CP157) and writes_persistence_boundary (CP158)
findings.recommended_action: Either consolidate to a single persistence key going forward or add a one-line note distinguishing 'executes' (runtime execution) from 'writes' (mutation) so the intent of keeping both is explicit.
findings.post_review_disposition: fixed: README and no-write attestation distinguish CP00-157 executes_persistence_boundary from CP00-158 writes_persistence_boundary.

parsed_review.p3_disposition: all four P3 findings fixed through code, validator, contract, or README updates
